title: When language models hallucinate, they leave "spilled energy" in their own math
summary: When large language models hallucinate, they leave measurable traces in their own computations. Researchers at the Sapienza University of Rome have developed a training-free method that picks up on these traces and generalizes better than previous approaches. title: Anthropic's new marketplace lets enterprise customers spend their existing AI budget on third-party tools
summary: Anthropic is launching the Anthropic Marketplace, a storefront where corporate customers can buy third-party software built on Anthropic's AI models. title: Trump administration drafts AI contract rules requiring companies to license systems for "all lawful use"
summary: New US guidelines for AI contracts would force companies to grant the government an irrevocable license for "all lawful use" and ban ideological bias in AI outputs, which is itself an ideological requirement with striking parallels to China's approach. title: OpenAI and Oracle stop expanding their flagship data center in Texas over power supply delays
summary: OpenAI and Oracle are halting the expansion of their Stargate data center in Texas. Instead, OpenAI plans to invest in Nvidia's next-gen Vera Rubin chips at new locations. title: Anthropic turns Claude Code into a background worker with local scheduled tasks
summary: Claude Code Desktop now lets users schedule recurring tasks that run automatically, like checking error logs and creating pull requests for fixable bugs every few hours. title: Anthropic's Claude Code subscription may consume up to $5,000 in compute per month while charging the user just $200
summary: Anthropic's $200 Claude Code subscription could consume up to $5,000 in compute per user, according to Cursor's internal analysis reported by Forbes.
